Invoked everytime the spline shape or the properties of the spline change.

Collection of curves in 3D space. Shape and behavior of the curves are controled through points , each with customizable handles, roll, scale, and up vectors. Two consecutive points define a segment/curve of the spline.

By adjusting the handles both smooth and sharp corners can be created. The spline can also be turned into a loop, combined with linear tangents this can be used to create polygons. Splines can also be used used for animations, camera movements, marking areas, or procedural geometry generation.
The position of the spline point.
Position of the In handle relative to the point position.
Position of the Out handle relative to the point position.

Describes how the spline should behave when entering/leaving a point.
The mmode and the handles In and Out position will determine the transition between segments.
Roll/Twist around the tangent axis.
X = Scale Length, Y = Scale Width, Z = Scale Height

Custom up vector at a spline point, can be used to calculate tangent frames (transforms) along the spline.
This allows fine grained control over the orientation of objects following the spline.

Point that defines part of the spline. Two consecutive points define a segment of the spline. The , / Handles and / properties are used to define the shape of the spline. P1 (Position) P1 (In) ▼ P1 (Out) o──────═══X═══──────o ───/ \─── ──/ \── -/ \- / \ | | P0 X X P2
